name: docker-mcp-server
description: Manage Docker through the Docker SDK for Python and the docker CLI: containers, images, networks, volumes, Compose, Swarm, Buildx, Scout, and OCI registries, exposed as MCP tools.
long_description: Exposes the Docker SDK for Python and the docker CLI (Compose, Buildx, Scout, Context, Stack) as MCP tools so an agent can manage local or remote Docker daemons. Connects over the local socket, a TCP endpoint, or `ssh://` (pure-Python paramiko transport, no system ssh binary). Read-only / no-destructive / disabled-domain switches are exposed below so you can register a restricted tool surface. The server runs entirely on your machine and sends no telemetry — see the Privacy Policy.

docker_host.title: DOCKER_HOST
docker_host.description: Docker daemon to connect to, e.g. unix:///var/run/docker.sock or ssh://user@host. Leave blank to use your default Docker context / socket.

read_only.title: Read-only mode
read_only.description: Register only read-only tools — no create, modify, or delete.

no_destructive.title: Disable destructive tools
no_destructive.description: Register everything except destructive tools (remove / prune / kill).

disable_domains.title: Disabled domains
disable_domains.description: Comma-separated tool domains to drop, e.g. swarm,plugins,scout.
